title: Unsafe eval usage
mock_patch:
diff --git a/solution.py b/solution.py
--- a/solution.py
+++ b/solution.py
@@ -1,6 +1,33 @@
 """Buggy expression calculator."""
+
+import ast
+
+
+ALLOWED_OPERATORS = {
+    ast.Add: lambda left, right: left + right,
+    ast.Sub: lambda left, right: left - right,
+    ast.Mult: lambda left, right: left * right,
+}
+
+
+def evaluate_node(node: ast.AST) -> int:
+    """Evaluate a restricted arithmetic AST."""
+    if isinstance(node, ast.Expression):
+        return evaluate_node(node.body)
+    if isinstance(node, ast.Constant) and isinstance(node.value, int):
+        return node.value
+    if isinstance(node, ast.BinOp):
+        operator = ALLOWED_OPERATORS.get(type(node.op))
+        if operator is None:
+            raise ValueError("unsupported operator")
+        return operator(evaluate_node(node.left), evaluate_node(node.right))
+    raise ValueError("unsupported expression")
 
 
 def calculate(expression: str) -> int:
     """Calculate a simple arithmetic expression."""
-    return int(eval(expression))
+    try:
+        parsed = ast.parse(expression, mode="eval")
+    except SyntaxError as error:
+        raise ValueError("invalid expression") from error
+    return evaluate_node(parsed)
